steps.title: Find the key comparison
steps.content:
The strongest signal in the passage is the explicit Mohs scale numbers and the rule that higher-numbered minerals can scratch lower-numbered ones.

The student’s goal is to highlight fluorite’s hardness in relation to talc and quartz, so the correct answer must directly compare fluorite’s scale number (4) to these other minerals.

steps.title: Apply the scale logic
steps.content:
Choice B correctly uses fluorite’s position (4) to show it can scratch talc (1) but not quartz (7), which aligns with the scale’s hierarchy.

The other three choices either omit fluorite entirely (C, D) or focus only on quartz’s relationships (A), missing the specific context needed to emphasize fluorite’s hardness.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
